text: OWASP A03:2021 Injection. Injection flaws occur when untrusted data is sent to an interpreter as part of a command or query. SQL injection, NoSQL injection, OS command injection, and LDAP injection are common attack vectors. Prevention includes using parameterized queries, ORMs, input validation, and least privilege database accounts. Prepared statements with variable binding are the most effective defense against SQL injection attacks.

OWASP A07:2021 Cross-Site Scripting (XSS). XSS flaws occur whenever an application includes untrusted data in a web page without proper validation or escaping. Three main types exist: Reflected XSS, Stored XSS, and DOM-based XSS. Prevention includes context-aware output encoding, Content Security Policy headers, and use of frameworks that auto-escape by default such as React or Angular.

CVE-2021-44228 (Log4Shell): Apache Log4j2 versions 2.0-beta9 through 2.15.0 (excluding security releases 2.12.2, 2.12.3, and 2.3.1) JNDI features used in configuration, log messages, and parameters do not protect against attacker-controlled LDAP and other JNDI related endpoints. An attacker who can control log messages or log message parameters can execute arbitrary code loaded from LDAP servers when message lookup substitution is enabled. This is a remote code execution vulnerability with CVSS score 10.0.

CVE-2017-5638: The Jakarta Multipart parser in Apache Struts 2 2.3.x before 2.3.32 and 2.5.x before 2.5.10.1 has incorrect exception handling and error-message generation during file-upload attempts, which allows remote attackers to execute arbitrary commands via a crafted Content-Type HTTP header. This command injection vulnerability was exploited in the Equifax data breach affecting 147 million consumers.

CVE-2019-0708 (BlueKeep): A remote code execution vulnerability exists in Remote Desktop Services when an unauthenticated attacker connects to the target system using RDP and sends specially crafted requests. This vulnerability is pre-authentication and requires no user interaction. An attacker who successfully exploited this vulnerability could execute arbitrary code on the target system and install programs, view, change, or delete data, or create new accounts with full user rights. This buffer overflow vulnerability has a CVSS score of 9.8.

CVE-2021-21972: The vSphere Client (HTML5) contains a remote code execution vulnerability in a vCenter Server plugin. A malicious actor with network access to port 443 may exploit this issue to execute commands with unrestricted privileges on the underlying operating system that hosts vCenter Server. This affects VMware vCenter Server before 6.5 U3n, 6.7 before 6.7 U3l, and 7.0 before 7.0 U1c. Path traversal combined with arbitrary file upload leads to remote code execution.

CVE-2023-34362: In Progress MOVEit Transfer before 2021.0.6, 2021.1.4, 2022.0.4, 2022.1.5, and 2023.0.1, a SQL injection vulnerability has been found in the MOVEit Transfer web application that could allow an unauthenticated attacker to gain access to MOVEit Transfer's database. Depending on the database engine being used (MySQL, Microsoft SQL Server, or Azure SQL), an attacker may be able to infer information about the structure and contents of the database and execute SQL statements that alter or delete database elements.

CVE-2020-1938 (Ghostcat): Apache Tomcat AJP connector has a file inclusion vulnerability. When using the Apache JServ Protocol (AJP), a specially crafted request can be used to read web application files from the server or to process any file in the web application as a JSP. If the web application allows file upload, this vulnerability allows remote code execution. Path traversal through AJP configuration allows reading arbitrary files from the server filesystem.

CVE-2015-7501: Red Hat JBoss Enterprise Application Platform (EAP) before 6.4.5 does not properly deserialize objects, which allows remote attackers to execute arbitrary code via crafted serialized Java objects. The Apache Commons Collections library contains classes that are susceptible to Java deserialization attacks. When an application deserializes untrusted data without validation, an attacker can craft payloads using gadget chains to achieve remote code execution.

CVE-2019-12384: FasterXML jackson-databind 2.x before 2.9.9.1 might allow attackers to have unspecified impact by leveraging failure to block the logback-core class from polymorphic deserialization. This is related to insecure deserialization where untrusted JSON data can be used to instantiate arbitrary Java classes through Jackson's polymorphic type handling. Mitigation involves disabling default typing, using @JsonTypeInfo with whitelisting, and upgrading to patched versions.

CVE-2018-3721: lodash node module before 4.17.5 is affected by a Prototype Pollution vulnerability via mergeWith, and lodash.merge functions that allow a malicious user to modify the prototype of Object via __proto__ causing the addition or modification of an existing property that will exist on all objects. Hardcoded secrets and improper validation of object properties can lead to prototype pollution in JavaScript applications.

CWE-89 Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an SQL Command (SQL Injection). The application constructs all or part of an SQL command using externally-influenced input from an upstream component, but it does not neutralize or incorrectly neutralizes special elements that could modify the intended SQL command. Without sufficient removal or quoting of SQL syntax in user-controllable inputs, the generated SQL query can cause those inputs to be interpreted as SQL instead of ordinary user data. Mitigation: use prepared statements and parameterized queries consistently.
